reasoning: The ORIGINAL and CORRECTED formalizations have identical theorem statements. Both use the same parameters (n : ℕ, d r : ℝ, x y z : EuclideanSpace ℝ (Fin n)), the same hypotheses (n ≥ 3, ‖x - y‖ = d, d > 0, r > 0, 2 * r > d), and the same conclusion (Set.Infinite of the set of points equidistant r from both x and y). The only differences are non-semantic: the corrected version opens an additional notation scope (InnerProductSpace) and increases maxHeartbeats — neither affects the theorem statement. The formalization faithfully captures the informal statement about infinitely many points z in ℝ^k equidistant from x and y when 2r > d > 0 and k ≥ 3.
